data.description:
## Finding

Future ChatGPT sessions may have the GitHub connector and repository visibility but lack a local clone, direct Gerrit access, shell network access, or a mounted Rebar tracker. Existing general agent guidance does not explain how to recognize this environment, what operations remain safe, or when ticket creation cannot be completed natively.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:27-45` requires work to be found, created, claimed, and recorded in Rebar before implementation.
- `AGENTS.md:124-131` states that GitHub is a read-only mirror and all code changes land through Gerrit.
- `templates/rebar-usage.md:18-30` requires search-before-create and native Rebar create/claim/link commands.
- `docs/concurrency.md:21-26` defines the tracker as a dedicated orphan `tickets` branch with append-only event files.
- `docs/concurrency.md:171-176` requires all writes to use the lock-guarded append/commit path; raw hand-authored tracker mutations are unsafe.

## Remediation

Add concise ChatGPT-specific guidance that teaches sessions to detect connector-limited execution, prefer the installed native Rebar CLI/MCP when a valid tracker worktree exists, respect the GitHub-read-only/Gerrit workflow, and avoid inventing tracker state or substituting GitHub Issues. Define a safe fallback that prepares fully formed ticket content and reports the exact missing capability rather than claiming tickets were created. Document the exceptional import path, if maintainers support one, for appending CLI-generated events without hand-authoring or mutating existing events.

Cascade flag: guidance must remain consistent with Rebar's append-only, optimistic-concurrency, and native-command invariants; it must not normalize direct contents-API writes as the ordinary workflow.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A ChatGPT-specific guidance file or clearly labeled section is discoverable from `AGENTS.md` without bloating the canonical startup context.
- [ ] Guidance includes an environment capability check covering local checkout, native `rebar`/`rebar-mcp`, tracker worktree, network/remote access, GitHub connector, and Gerrit availability.
- [ ] Guidance states that the GitHub repository is a read-only mirror for code changes and that GitHub Issues are not a substitute for the Rebar tracker.
- [ ] Guidance requires search-before-create and native parent/child creation whenever the live tracker is accessible.
- [ ] Guidance prohibits hand-authoring, modifying, or deleting event files and explains the append-only reason.
- [ ] When native ticket creation is impossible, guidance requires producing reviewable ticket payloads plus a transparent capability-gap report, without claiming persistence occurred.
- [ ] Any documented exceptional import mechanism accepts only native CLI-generated events, verifies the target branch head, appends new unique files, and runs tracker validation afterward.
- [ ] A lightweight documentation test verifies that ChatGPT guidance remains linked and does not contradict canonical Rebar or Gerrit rules.
data.title: Document safe ChatGPT workflows for connector-limited Rebar environments
